stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 30 factory workers. The sample mean is 331.4 sec with a sample standard deviation of 72.2 sec. However, 3 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 27 observations have a mean of 309.0 sec and standard deviation of 44.6 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 331.4, 95% CI = [304.4, 358.3]. Without outliers: mean = 309.0, 95% CI = [291.8, 326.2]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [291.8, 358.3]. Point estimate: 320.2 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [291.8, 358.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
